example_triple.subject: iPhone 15 Plus
example_triple.object: Black, Blue, Green, Yellow, and Pink

example_triple.subject: iPhone 15 Plus
example_triple.object: Black, Blue, Green, Yellow, and Pink

generated_text_by_instance.text: The Parla Sonic 3.5 is a discontinued entry-level Android 4.1 smartphone released in late 2013. It features a compact 3.5-inch TFT display (320x480), a Spreadtrum SC7710G chipset with a 1.0 GHz CPU, and minimal memory with 512MB of RAM. The device includes a 2MP main camera, Dual SIM support, and is powered by a removable 1300 mAh battery.

The Karbonn Titanium Hexa is a discontinued Android 4.4.2 smartphone released in April 2014. It features a 5.5-inch Full HD IPS LCD display, a hexa-core 1.5 GHz processor, 2GB of RAM, and 16GB of internal storage. It is equipped with a 13MP main camera, a 5MP selfie camera, and a 2050 mAh battery, priced at approximately 200 EUR.

The QMobile E1 is a discontinued Android 6.0 smartphone released in January 2017. It features a 5.0-inch IPS LCD display (720x1280), a Mediatek MT6735P quad-core processor, 3GB of RAM, and 16GB of internal storage. Notable hardware includes an 8MP main camera, a 5MP selfie camera, and a large 4000 mAh removable Li-Po battery.

The Garmin-Asus nuvifone A50 is a discontinued Android 2.1 smartphone released in 2010. It features a 3.5-inch TFT display (320x480), a Snapdragon S1 processor (600 MHz), 256MB RAM, and 4GB of internal storage. Key highlights include integrated Garmin navigation, a 3.15 MP main camera, and a removable 1150 mAh battery.

Released in May 2023, the Infinix Note 30 5G is a budget-friendly smartphone featuring a 6.78-inch 120Hz IPS LCD display and a Mediatek Dimensity 6080 chipset. It stands out with a high-resolution 108MP triple main camera, a 5000mAh battery with 45W fast charging, and JBL-tuned dual speakers. Available in configurations up to 256GB storage and 8GB RAM, it runs Android 13 and supports 5G connectivity.

The Asus ROG Phone 6 Diablo Immortal Edition is a high-performance gaming smartphone released in November 2022. It features a Snapdragon 8+ Gen 1 chipset, 16GB RAM, and 512GB storage, paired with a 6.78-inch 165Hz AMOLED display. Designed for gamers, it includes pressure-sensitive triggers, an illuminated RGB logo, and a massive 6000mAh battery with 65W fast charging. The device also boasts a 50MP triple camera system capable of 8K video recording and dual USB-C ports.

Released in August 2023, the Samsung Galaxy F34 is a 5G-enabled smartphone featuring a 6.5-inch Super AMOLED display with a 120Hz refresh rate. It is powered by an Exynos 1280 chipset, available with 6GB or 8GB of RAM and 128GB of storage. Key highlights include a high-capacity 6000 mAh battery with 25W charging, a triple rear camera setup led by a 50MP OIS main sensor, and a price point of approximately 210 EUR.

Released in August 2021, the Micromax In 2b is an affordable Android 11 smartphone priced around 90 EUR. It features a 6.52-inch IPS LCD display, a Unisoc T610 octa-core chipset, and 64GB of storage with either 4GB or 6GB of RAM. Notable hardware includes a large 5000 mAh battery with 10W charging, a 13MP dual rear camera, a 5MP selfie camera, and a rear-mounted fingerprint sensor.

The Philips S396 is a discontinued dual-SIM smartphone released in June 2015. It features a 5.0-inch IPS LCD display (720x1280), is powered by a Mediatek MT6735P quad-core processor running Android 5.1, and has limited memory with 1GB RAM and 8GB internal storage. It is equipped with 8MP front and rear cameras, a removable 2300 mAh battery, and supports LTE connectivity.

The Prestigio MultiPhone 7600 Duo is a discontinued dual-SIM Android 4.2 smartphone released in early 2014. It features a large 6.0-inch IPS LCD display (720x1280), a quad-core 1.5 GHz CPU, and minimal memory with 1GB RAM and 4GB internal storage. It is equipped with a 13MP main camera, an 8MP selfie camera, and a removable 2600 mAh battery, priced at approximately 270 EUR.

The Cubot Tab KingKong is a rugged 10.1-inch Android 13 tablet released in August 2023. It features a durable IP68/IP69K dust and water-resistant design, weighing 820g. Key specifications include a Mediatek MT8788 octa-core processor, 8GB RAM, 256GB internal storage, and a massive 10,600 mAh battery with 18W wired charging. It is equipped with a 1200x1920 IPS LCD display, a 16MP main camera, and an 8MP selfie camera.

The Honor V Purse is a foldable 5G smartphone released in September 2023, featuring a 7.71-inch main OLED display and a 6.45-inch cover display, both with 90Hz refresh rates and 1600 nits peak brightness. Powered by the Snapdragon 778G 5G chipset and 16GB of RAM, it offers storage options of 256GB or 512GB. It includes a 50MP dual main camera system capable of 4K video, an 8MP selfie camera, and a 4500 mAh battery with 35W wired charging. Priced at approximately 770 EUR, it runs Android 13 with MagicOS 7.2.

The Archos Oxygen 68XL is a budget-friendly Android 9.0 smartphone released in May 2019, priced at approximately 150 EUR. Its most notable feature is a large 6.85-inch IPS LCD display paired with a substantial 5000 mAh battery. It is powered by a Mediatek Helio P22 chipset with 3GB RAM and 32GB of internal storage. The device features a basic dual rear camera (8MP main) and a 5MP selfie camera, with a rear-mounted fingerprint sensor for security.

The O2 XDA Comet is a discontinued Windows Mobile 6.0 Professional smartphone released in September 2007. It features a 2.7-inch resistive touchscreen, an Intel XScale 620 MHz CPU, 64MB RAM, and 1GB ROM. Connectivity includes GSM/HSPA, Wi-Fi, Bluetooth 1.2, and an infrared port. It is equipped with a 2MP main camera, a VGA selfie camera, and a removable 1350mAh Li-Ion battery.

Released in October 2023, the Oppo A79 is a 5G-enabled smartphone featuring a 6.72-inch 90Hz IPS LCD display and a Dimensity 6020 chipset. It comes with 8GB RAM, 128GB of UFS 2.2 storage, and a 50MP dual main camera. The device is powered by a 5000 mAh battery with 33W fast charging and is IPX4 splash resistant.

The ZTE Blade A73 5G, released in September 2023, is an affordable 5G smartphone featuring a 6.52-inch 90Hz IPS LCD display. It is powered by a Unisoc T760 chipset with 4GB RAM and 128GB storage, running Android 13. Key hardware includes a 50MP dual main camera, a 5MP selfie camera, and a large 5000 mAh battery. It costs approximately €129.00.

The Sagem Puma Phone is a discontinued 2010 device featuring a 2.8-inch resistive touchscreen, a 3.15 MP main camera, and a VGA selfie camera. Notable for its sports-centric features, it includes an integrated solar cell, a pedometer, and bike/run trackers. It runs on GSM/HSPA networks and is powered by a removable 880 mAh battery.

Released in November 2019, the Cat S52 is a rugged smartphone designed for durability, featuring IP68 water/dust resistance, MIL-STD-810G compliance, and drop resistance up to 1.5m. It features a 5.65-inch IPS LCD (720 x 1440), a Helio P35 chipset, 4GB RAM, and 64GB storage. The device is equipped with a 12MP main camera, an 8MP selfie camera, and a 3100 mAh battery with 10W charging, priced at approximately 440 EUR.

The Oppo Reno11 (China), released in late 2023, is a 5G smartphone featuring a 6.7-inch 120Hz OLED display with HDR10+ and a peak brightness of 950 nits. It is powered by the Mediatek Dimensity 8200 chipset, running Android 14, with memory options up to 12GB RAM and 512GB UFS 3.1 storage. The device boasts a triple main camera setup led by a 50MP OIS wide lens and a 32MP selfie camera, both capable of 4K video. It is equipped with a 4800 mAh battery supporting 67W fast charging and is priced at approximately 320 EUR.

The Panasonic P85 Nxt is an entry-level Android 7.1.2 smartphone released in November 2018. It features a 5.0-inch IPS LCD display (720p) protected by Gorilla Glass, and is powered by a Snapdragon 210 quad-core processor with 2GB RAM and 16GB of internal storage. Notable highlights include a large 4000 mAh battery, dual SIM support, and a rear-mounted fingerprint sensor. It is equipped with an 8MP main camera and a 5MP selfie camera, priced at approximately 7000 INR.

The BenQ-Siemens EF61 is a discontinued GSM phone released in July 2006. It is a compact device weighing 99g, featuring a primary 128x160 TFT display, a secondary external CSTN screen, and a 1.3 MP main camera. Key technical specs include 1.5MB of internal memory, a microSD slot, Bluetooth connectivity, and a removable 750 mAh battery providing up to 3 hours of talk time.

The Alcatel 1B (2022) is an entry-level smartphone released in May 2022, priced around 100 EUR. It features a 5.5-inch TFT LCD display (720x1440), a Mediatek Helio A22 chipset, 2GB of RAM, and 32GB of internal storage running Android 11 (Go edition). It is equipped with an 8MP main camera, a 5MP selfie camera, and a removable 3000 mAh battery.

Released in July 2019, the T-Mobile Revvlry is a budget-friendly Android 9.0 smartphone priced around 180 EUR. It features a 5.7-inch IPS LCD display (720 x 1520), a Snapdragon 632 chipset, 3GB of RAM, and 32GB of internal storage. The device is equipped with a 13MP main camera, an 8MP selfie camera, a 3000 mAh battery with 10W charging, and is splash-resistant.

The Honor X50i+, released in November 2023, is a slim 5G smartphone featuring a 6.7-inch 90Hz AMOLED display with a peak brightness of 2000 nits. It is powered by the Mediatek Dimensity 6080 chipset, 12GB of RAM, and storage options of 256GB or 512GB. Key highlights include a high-resolution 108MP main camera, a 4500 mAh battery with 35W wired charging, and a lightweight design at 166g, priced around 210 EUR.

Released in May 2019, the Vodafone Smart N10 is an entry-level Android 9.0 smartphone priced around 150 EUR. It features a 5.67-inch IPS LCD display, a Mediatek Helio A22 quad-core processor, and modest memory with 2GB RAM and 16GB internal storage. The device is equipped with a 13MP main camera, an 8MP selfie camera, and is powered by a 2920 mAh non-removable battery.

The Philips PH1, released in December 2021, is an affordable Android smartphone priced at approximately 70 EUR. It features a 6.51-inch IPS LCD display (720 x 1440), a Unisoc Tiger T310 quad-core chipset, 4GB of RAM, and storage options ranging from 32GB to 128GB. The device is equipped with a 13MP dual main camera, a 5MP selfie camera, and a 4700 mAh battery, supporting LTE connectivity and USB Type-C.

The Sony Ericsson Xperia active is a discontinued, rugged Android smartphone released in October 2011. It features a compact 3.0-inch LED-backlit LCD, IP67 dust and water resistance, and a built-in flashlight. Powered by a Snapdragon S2 processor with 512MB RAM, it includes a 5MP main camera capable of 720p video and runs on Android 2.3 (upgradable to 4.0). The device is powered by a removable 1200 mAh battery.

The Sharp Aquos V6, released in February 2022, is an entry-level Android 12 smartphone priced around 130 EUR. It features a 6.52-inch IPS LCD display (720x1600), a Mediatek Helio G25 chipset, 3GB of RAM, and 32GB of internal storage. Notable hardware includes a triple rear camera setup led by a 13MP wide lens, a 5MP selfie camera, and a large 5000 mAh battery with USB Power Delivery 3.0.

The OnePlus Nord CE 3 Lite is a budget-friendly 5G smartphone released in April 2023. It features a large 6.72-inch 120Hz IPS LCD display, is powered by a Snapdragon 695 5G chipset with 8GB of RAM, and offers storage options of 128GB or 256GB. Its standout hardware includes a 108MP main triple-camera system, a 5000mAh battery with fast 67W wired charging, and stereo speakers with a 3.5mm jack.

The Archos Oxygen 63, released in May 2019, is an entry-level Android 9.0 smartphone priced around 130 EUR. It features a 6.26-inch IPS LCD display (720x1520), a Unisoc SC9863 octa-core processor, 4GB of RAM, and 64GB of internal storage. Notable hardware includes a dual rear camera (8MP main), a 5MP selfie camera, a rear-mounted fingerprint sensor, and a removable 3000 mAh battery.

The Parla Mist is a discontinued GSM feature phone released in late 2012. It features a compact design with a QWERTY keyboard, a small 1.8-inch TFT display, and the notable ability to support Triple SIM cards. Basic hardware includes a VGA main camera with LED flash, a 700 mAh removable battery, and a microSDHC slot for expandable storage. Connectivity is limited to Bluetooth 2.1 and microUSB, with no WLAN or NFC support.

Released in April 2019, the Allview Soul X6 Xtreme is an Android 9.0 smartphone featuring a 6.39-inch AMOLED display with Gorilla Glass 5 and an under-display fingerprint sensor. It is powered by a Mediatek Helio P70 chipset with 4GB RAM and 64GB storage. Notable features include a motorized pop-up 8MP selfie camera, a 16MP dual main camera, and a 3450 mAh battery supporting 18W wired and wireless charging. It was priced at approximately 360 EUR.

The i-mobile Hitz 2206 is a discontinued dual-SIM GSM phone released in 2010. It features a QWERTY keyboard, a 220 x 176 pixel TFT display, and a basic 0.3MP main camera. The device includes a stereo FM radio and a removable 1000 mAh battery offering up to 667 hours of standby time, but lacks Bluetooth, WLAN, USB, and a 3.5mm jack.

The Eten glofiish X900 is a discontinued Windows Mobile 6.1 Professional smartphone released in June 2008. It features a 2.8-inch resistive touchscreen with a 480x640 resolution, a Samsung 533 MHz CPU, 128MB RAM, and a 3.15 MP main camera. Connectivity includes HSPA, Wi-Fi, Bluetooth 2.0, and GPS, powered by a removable 1300mAh Li-Ion battery.

Released in July 2022, the Yezz Max 2 Ultra is an Android 11 smartphone featuring a 6.1-inch IPS LCD display (600 x 1280 pixels). It is powered by a Mediatek MT6737M quad-core processor with 2GB of RAM and 32GB of internal storage. The device includes a 13MP dual main camera, an 8MP selfie camera, and a removable 3000 mAh battery with USB Power Delivery. Additional features include Dual SIM support, a rear-mounted fingerprint sensor, and a 3.5mm headphone jack.

The Tel.Me. T939 was a Windows PocketPC 2003 Phone edition device announced in Q1 2004, though it was ultimately cancelled. It featured a 160x240 TFT resistive touchscreen, a 2MP autofocus camera with LED flash, and an 800mAh removable Li-Ion battery. Notable capabilities included an infrared port, MP3 player, and direct print functionality, but it lacked Bluetooth, WLAN, and a 3.5mm jack.

The LG K52, released in November 2020, is a budget-friendly smartphone featuring a 6.6-inch IPS LCD display and a Mediatek Helio P35 chipset. It is equipped with 4GB of RAM, 64GB of internal storage, and a 4000 mAh battery. Notable hardware includes a quad-camera system led by a 48MP main sensor, a 13MP selfie camera, and MIL-STD-810G compliance for added durability. It runs Android 10 and is priced at approximately 220 EUR.

The Wind DUO 2000 is a discontinued dual-SIM GSM phone released in March 2007. It features a compact 1.5-inch OLED display, Bluetooth 1.2, and an MP3 player, but lacks a camera, WLAN, and a 3.5mm jack. It is powered by a removable 1660 mAh Li-Po battery providing up to 3 hours of talk time.

The Modu Sport jacket is a discontinued 2009 GSM device featuring a small 1.3-inch AMOLED display and 1.6GB of internal memory. Designed for activity, it includes a built-in pedometer, sport application, and MP3 player, powered by a removable 530 mAh Li-Po battery.

Released in September 2023, the Doogee T10Plus is an Android 13 tablet featuring a 10.5-inch IPS LCD display (1200 x 1920) and stylus support. It is powered by a Unisoc T606 octa-core chipset with 8GB RAM and 256GB of internal storage. The device includes a 13MP dual main camera, an 8MP selfie camera, quad stereo speakers, and a large 8250 mAh battery with 18W wired charging. It is built with an aluminum frame and back, weighing 505g.

The Sony Ericsson Xperia mini pro is a discontinued compact smartphone released in August 2011. It features a 3.0-inch LED-backlit LCD, a physical QWERTY keyboard, and runs on Android (up to version 4.0) powered by a 1.0 GHz Snapdragon S2 processor. Key specs include 512MB RAM, a 5MP main camera with 720p video, and a removable 1200 mAh battery.

The Nvidia Shield is a discontinued 8-inch Android tablet released in July 2014. It features a 1920x1200 IPS LCD display, an Nvidia Tegra K1 chipset with a quad-core 2.2 GHz CPU, and 2GB of RAM with 16GB of internal storage. Key highlights include a stylus, HDMI port, stereo speakers with dual bass reflex ports, and a 5400 mAh battery. It lacks cellular connectivity and was priced at approximately 230 EUR.

Released in September 2023, the Doogee S41 Pro is a rugged Android 12 smartphone featuring IP68/IP69K water and dust resistance, MIL-STD-810H compliance, and drop resistance up to 1.8m. It is equipped with a 5.5-inch IPS LCD (720x1440), a Helio A22 quad-core processor, 4GB RAM, and 64GB of internal storage. Notable highlights include a massive 6300 mAh battery and a triple rear camera system led by a 13MP sensor.

The Posh Volt Max LTE L640 is a discontinued Android 5.1 smartphone released in June 2016. It features a large 6.44-inch IPS LCD display (1280x1920) and is powered by an octa-core Mediatek MT6753 chipset with 3GB of RAM and storage options of 32GB or 64GB. Notable hardware includes a massive 6000 mAh battery, a 13MP main camera, and an 8MP selfie camera.

The VK Mobile VK4000 is a discontinued GSM phone launched in March 2006. This compact black device features a 1.8-inch TFT display, 128MB of internal memory, and a basic VGA main camera. It supports Bluetooth, Java, and MP3 playback, and is powered by a removable 800mAh Li-Ion battery. It was priced at approximately 60 EUR.

Released in November 2023, the itel A70 is an entry-level Android 13 (Go edition) smartphone featuring a 6.6-inch IPS LCD display and a Unisoc T603 octa-core chipset. It offers flexible memory options with up to 256GB storage and 4GB RAM, powered by a large 5000 mAh battery with 10W charging. The device includes a 13MP dual rear camera, an 8MP selfie camera, and a side-mounted fingerprint sensor.

The Sendo X is a discontinued GSM phone announced in Q4 2003. It features a 2.2-inch TFT display (176 x 220 pixels), runs on Symbian 6.1 with a 120 MHz ARM925T CPU, and has 32MB of internal memory expandable via MMC/SD. Key hardware includes a VGA main camera with LED flash, Bluetooth 1.1, an infrared port, and a removable 1050 mAh Li-Po battery providing up to 7 hours of talk time.

The Realme Narzo 60x is a budget-friendly 5G smartphone released in September 2023, priced at ₹12,999. It features a large 6.72-inch 120Hz IPS LCD display and is powered by the Mediatek Dimensity 6100+ chipset with 4GB or 6GB of RAM and 128GB of storage. Key hardware includes a 50MP dual main camera, an 8MP selfie camera, and a substantial 5000mAh battery supporting 33W fast charging. It runs Android 13 and includes a side-mounted fingerprint sensor and a 3.5mm headphone jack.

The LG K92 5G, released in November 2020, is a 5G-enabled smartphone featuring a large 6.7-inch IPS LCD display (1080 x 2400). It is powered by a Snapdragon 690 5G chipset with 6GB RAM and 128GB of internal storage. Notable hardware includes a quad-camera system led by a 64MP main sensor capable of 4K video, a 16MP selfie camera, and a 4000mAh battery with Quick Charge 4.0. Additional features include stereo speakers, a side-mounted fingerprint sensor, and NFC support.

The Wind Van Gogh 2100 was a compact GSM phone announced in October 2007 but ultimately cancelled. It featured a 1.8-inch TFT display, a 2MP main camera, and 128MB of internal memory with microSD expansion. Notable features included a detachable Bluetooth earpiece and a removable 820 mAh battery.

The Spice Smart Flo 358 (Mi-358) is a discontinued entry-level Android 4.4.2 smartphone released in October 2014. It features a compact 3.5-inch TFT display (320x480), a dual-core 1.3 GHz CPU, and very limited memory with 256MB RAM and 512MB internal storage. It includes a 2MP main camera with LED flash and a 1.3MP selfie camera, powered by a 1450 mAh Li-Ion battery.

The Motorola Edge (2023) is a 5G-enabled smartphone priced at $349.99, featuring a 6.6-inch P-OLED display with a smooth 144Hz refresh rate and HDR10+ support. It is powered by a MediaTek Dimensity 7030 chipset with 8GB RAM and 256GB of internal storage. Notable hardware includes an IP68 water-resistant build with an eco-leather back, a 50MP main camera with OIS, and a 32MP selfie camera. The device is equipped with a 4400 mAh battery supporting fast 68W wired and 15W wireless charging.

The Huawei Watch Ultimate Design is a luxury smartwatch released in October 2023, priced at approximately 4,360 EUR. It features premium materials including an 18k gold bezel and crown, sapphire crystal, and a zircon-based amorphous alloy case. The device boasts a 1.5-inch LTPO AMOLED display, runs HarmonyOS 4.0, and is highly durable with IP68 and 10 ATM water resistance (suitable for diving). Key technical specs include a 530 mAh battery with wireless charging, comprehensive health sensors (ECG, SpO2, body temperature), and multi-system GPS, though it lacks cellular connectivity.

The Plum Optimax 13 is an entry-level 10.1-inch Android 8.0 (Go edition) tablet released in early 2019. It features a 1280x800 IPS LCD display, a Spreadtrum SC7731 quad-core processor, and minimal memory with 1GB RAM and 8GB internal storage. It is equipped with a 5MP main camera, a 2MP selfie camera, and a 4700 mAh battery, supporting Dual SIM connectivity and HSPA speeds.

The Innostream INNO 36 was a compact GSM phone announced in Q2 2005 but ultimately cancelled. It featured a dual-display setup (a main TFT and a secondary external STN screen), a VGA camera, and basic connectivity including infrared and WAP 1.2.1, powered by a 920mAh removable Li-Ion battery.

The Toshiba Excite 10 SE is a discontinued 10.1-inch Android 4.1 tablet released in December 2012. It features a 1280x800 LED-backlit IPS LCD, an Nvidia Tegra 3 quad-core processor, 1GB of RAM, and 16GB of internal storage. It lacks cellular connectivity but includes Wi-Fi, Bluetooth 3.0, and GPS. The device is equipped with a 3.15 MP main camera, a 1.2 MP selfie camera, stereo speakers with SRS audio, and a Li-Po battery providing up to 12 hours of multimedia use.

The Unnecto Neo V is a discontinued Android 5.1 smartphone released in July 2016. It features a 5.0-inch IPS LCD display (480 x 854 pixels), a quad-core 1.3 GHz CPU, and basic memory with 1GB RAM and 8GB internal storage. It is equipped with an 8MP main camera, a 1.3MP selfie camera, and a removable 2000 mAh battery.

The Posh Optima LTE L530 is a discontinued Android 5.1 smartphone released in April 2016. It features a 5.0-inch IPS LCD display (720x1280), an octa-core 1.3 GHz processor, 2GB of RAM, and 16GB of internal storage. Notable hardware includes matching 13MP front and rear cameras, Dual SIM capability, and a 3000 mAh non-removable battery.

The Dell Streak 7 Wi-Fi is a discontinued 7-inch Android tablet released in April 2011. It features a 480 x 800 TFT display with Gorilla Glass, powered by an Nvidia Tegra 2 dual-core processor and 16GB of internal storage. Notably, it lacks cellular connectivity, relying on Wi-Fi, and includes a 5MP main camera and a 2780 mAh battery. It launched at approximately 140 EUR.

Released in September 2023, the Cubot Note 21 is an affordable budget smartphone priced around $79.99/€99.99. It features a 6.56-inch 90Hz IPS LCD display, is powered by a Unisoc T606 chipset with 6GB RAM and 128GB storage, and runs Android 13. Key hardware includes a 50MP dual main camera, an 8MP selfie camera, and a large 5200 mAh battery with 10W wired charging.

The MWg Atom Life is a discontinued Windows Mobile 6 Professional smartphone released in January 2007. It features a 2.7-inch resistive touchscreen, an Intel XScale PXA 270 624 MHz CPU, 64MB RAM, and 1GB ROM. The device includes a 2MP main camera with LED flash, a VGA selfie camera, and is powered by a removable 1530 mAh battery.

The itel Smart Watch 2, released in 2023, is a basic black smartwatch featuring a 1.8-inch IPS LCD display and an IP68-rated plastic build. It runs on a proprietary OS and includes health sensors for heart rate, SpO2, and acceleration, powered by a 230 mAh battery. It lacks cellular connectivity, Wi-Fi, and a camera, relying on Bluetooth 5.1 for communications.

The Sony Ericsson WT18i is a discontinued Android-based smartphone released in 2011. It features a compact 3.2-inch TFT display, an 806 MHz Marvell CPU, and limited internal storage of 150MB, expandable via microSDHC. It is equipped with a 3.15 MP main camera, a selfie camera, and a removable 1200 mAh battery. Notable features include a Walkman player and a price point of approximately 230 EUR.

The VK Mobile VK7000 is a cancelled 2006 mobile phone featuring a 1.8-inch TFT display, a 1.3 MP main camera, and a VGA selfie camera. It supports GSM/UMTS networks, Bluetooth, and Java, and is powered by a removable 800mAh Li-Ion battery.

Released in October 2018, the Panasonic Eluga X1 Pro is a mid-range smartphone featuring a 6.18-inch IPS LCD display (1080 x 2246) and a premium build with glass front/back and a stainless steel frame. It is powered by a Mediatek Helio P60 chipset with options for 4GB or 6GB of RAM and 64GB or 128GB of storage. The device includes a 16MP dual main camera, a 16MP selfie camera, and a 3000 mAh battery supporting wireless charging. Notable features include Infrared Face ID and an infrared port, though it lacks a 3.5mm headphone jack.

The AT&T Quickfire is a discontinued QWERTY-keyboard phone released in November 2008. It features a 2.8-inch TFT display, a 1.3 MP main camera, and limited internal memory of 29MB, expandable via microSD. Connectivity is basic, supporting GSM/HSPA networks and Bluetooth 1.2, but lacking WLAN and a 3.5mm jack. It is powered by a removable 1200 mAh battery.

The Fairphone 4 is a 5G-enabled smartphone released in October 2021, featuring a 6.3-inch IPS LCD display and a Snapdragon 750G 5G chipset. It offers memory configurations of 128GB/6GB or 256GB/8GB RAM and is equipped with a dual 48MP main camera system and a 25MP selfie camera. Notable for its durability and sustainability, it is MIL-STD-810G compliant, IP54 rated, and includes a removable 3905 mAh battery and a standard 5-year warranty.

The iPhone 15 Plus, released in September 2023, features a 6.7-inch Super Retina XDR OLED display with a peak brightness of 2000 nits and is powered by the Apple A16 Bionic chip with 6GB of RAM. It offers storage options up to 512GB and a versatile dual-camera system headlined by a 48MP wide lens. The device is IP68 water-resistant, uses a USB Type-C port, and is powered by a 4383 mAh battery supporting MagSafe wireless charging. It is available in Black, Blue, Green, Yellow, and Pink, with a starting price of approximately $929.99.

The Alcatel 1L Pro (2021) is an entry-level Android 11 (Go edition) smartphone released in June 2021, priced around 110 EUR. It features a 6.1-inch IPS LCD display (720 x 1560), a Unisoc SC9863A octa-core chipset, and basic memory specs with 2GB RAM and 32GB internal storage. For photography, it has a 13MP dual rear camera and a 5MP selfie camera, both supporting 1080p video. The device is powered by a 3000 mAh battery and includes a rear-mounted fingerprint sensor, FM radio, and a 3.5mm headphone jack.

Released in February 2018, the verykool s5036 Apollo is an entry-level Android 7.0 smartphone featuring a 5.0-inch IPS LCD display and a Mediatek MT6580M quad-core processor. It has limited memory with 8GB internal storage and 1GB RAM, a dual rear camera (8MP + 2MP), a 5MP selfie camera, and a removable 2000 mAh battery. Additional features include Dual SIM support and a rear-mounted fingerprint sensor.

The BenQ C36 is a discontinued GSM feature phone released in April 2008. It features a compact 2.0-inch TFT display, a 1.3 MP main camera, and a removable 640 mAh battery providing up to 2 hours and 40 minutes of talk time. Notable features include dedicated music keys, dual loudspeakers, an MP3 player, and a microSD slot, though it lacks Bluetooth, WLAN, and a selfie camera.

The Vodafone Smart V8 is a discontinued Android 7.1.1 smartphone released in June 2017. It features a 5.5-inch Full HD IPS LCD display, a Snapdragon 435 octa-core processor, 3GB of RAM, and 32GB of internal storage. For photography, it is equipped with a 16MP main camera and an 8MP selfie camera. The device is powered by a 3000 mAh battery and includes a rear-mounted fingerprint sensor and NFC support.

The Acer Iconia Tab 10 A3-A40 is a discontinued 10.1-inch Android 6.0 tablet released in June 2016. It features a 1920 x 1200 IPS LCD display, a quad-core 1.3 GHz processor, and 2GB of RAM with storage options ranging from 16GB to 64GB. Notable hardware includes quad stereo speakers, a 6100 mAh battery, and a 5MP main camera, though it lacks cellular connectivity and NFC.

The Maxwest Gravity 5 LTE is a discontinued entry-level smartphone released in February 2016. It features a 5.0-inch IPS LCD screen (720x1280), runs Android 5.1 on a Snapdragon 210 quad-core processor, and has minimal memory with 8GB internal storage and 1GB RAM. It includes an 8MP main camera, a 2MP selfie camera, and a removable 2200 mAh battery.

Released in January 2023, the TCL Ion X is an entry-level Android smartphone priced at approximately 100 EUR. It features a 6.0-inch IPS LCD display (720x1440), is powered by a Mediatek Helio G25 chipset with 3GB RAM and 32GB storage, and includes a 13MP main camera and a 5MP selfie camera. It is equipped with a removable 3000 mAh battery supporting 10W wired charging.

The Spice Smart Flo 503 (Mi-503) is a discontinued entry-level Android 4.4.2 smartphone released in August 2014. It features a 5.0-inch IPS LCD display (480 x 854 pixels), a dual-core 1.3 GHz processor, and minimal memory with 512MB RAM and 4GB internal storage. The device includes an 8MP main camera, a 2MP selfie camera, and is powered by a 2000 mAh Li-Ion battery.

Released in September 2011, the Sony Ericsson Xperia Arc S is a discontinued Android smartphone featuring a 4.2-inch LED-backlit LCD (480 x 854 pixels) and a lightweight 117g design. It is powered by a 1.4 GHz Snapdragon S2 chipset with 512MB RAM and 1GB internal storage (expandable via microSDHC). Key features include an 8MP main camera with 720p video recording, a removable 1500 mAh battery, and an HDMI port, though it lacks a selfie camera.

Released in November 2023, the Honor 100 is a 5G smartphone featuring a high-brightness 6.7-inch 120Hz OLED display (2600 nits peak). It is powered by the Snapdragon 7 Gen 3 chipset with up to 16GB RAM and 512GB storage. The device boasts a high-resolution 50MP main camera with OIS and a 50MP selfie camera, both capable of 4K video. It is equipped with a 5000mAh battery supporting fast 100W wired charging and is priced at approximately 320 EUR.

Released in July 2023, the Tecno Pova 5 is a budget-friendly smartphone featuring a large 6.78-inch 120Hz IPS LCD display and a Mediatek Helio G99 chipset. It comes with 8GB of RAM and storage options of 128GB or 256GB. Notable highlights include a massive 6000 mAh battery with 45W fast charging, a 50MP main camera, and stereo speakers, all priced at ₹11,999.

The Sagem my721z is a discontinued 2G GSM phone released in February 2008. This compact silver device features a 2.2-inch TFT display, a 3.15 MP main camera, and a removable 1000mAh Li-Ion battery providing up to 3 hours of talk time. It includes basic connectivity like Bluetooth and FM radio, lacks a selfie camera and 3.5mm jack, and supports microSD expansion.

Released in June 2023, the BLU G73 is an affordable Android 13 smartphone (approx. 140 EUR) featuring a large 6.8-inch 90Hz IPS LCD display and a durable eco-leather back. It is equipped with 128GB storage, 6GB RAM, and a long-lasting 5000mAh battery with 18W wired charging. The device features a triple rear camera system led by a 50MP wide lens and an 8MP selfie camera, both supporting 1080p video.

Released in April 2017, the BlackBerry Keyone is a productivity-focused smartphone featuring a capacitive touch QWERTY keyboard and a 4.5-inch IPS LCD display (1080 x 1620). It is powered by a Snapdragon 625 chipset with options for 3GB or 4GB of RAM and 32GB or 64GB of storage. Key hardware includes a 12MP main camera capable of 4K video, an 8MP selfie camera, and a 3500 mAh battery with 18W fast charging. It originally launched with Android 7.1 (upgradable to 8.1) and was priced around 290 EUR.

Released in June 2021, the Gionee Smartwatch 7 is a budget-friendly wearable priced at approximately 50 EUR. It features a 1.3-inch TFT LCD display (240 x 240 pixels), IP67 water resistance, and health sensors for heart rate, SpO2, and acceleration. The device runs on a proprietary OS, connects via Bluetooth 4.0, and is powered by a 130 mAh battery.

Released in March 2021, the Coolpad Cool 10A is an entry-level Android 11 smartphone priced around 90 EUR. It features a 6.52-inch IPS LCD display (720 x 1600), a Unisoc SC9863A octa-core chipset, 2GB RAM, and 64GB of internal storage. The device is equipped with a 13MP dual rear camera, a 5MP selfie camera, and a 4000 mAh battery with 10W wired charging. Notable hardware includes a rear-mounted fingerprint sensor, microUSB port, and a dedicated microSDXC slot.

Released in December 2022, the Wiko 5G is a HarmonyOS smartphone featuring a 6.78-inch 120Hz IPS LCD display and a Snapdragon 695 5G chipset. It is equipped with 8GB of RAM and either 128GB or 256GB of storage. Notable highlights include a high-resolution 108MP quad-camera system, a 16MP selfie camera, and a 4000mAh battery with fast 66W wired charging. The device is splash-resistant and priced at approximately 270 EUR.

The Philips I908 is a discontinued Dual SIM smartphone released in August 2015. It features a 5.0-inch Full HD IPS LCD display, an octa-core 1.7 GHz processor, 2GB of RAM, and 16GB of internal storage. It is equipped with a 13MP main camera capable of 1080p video, a 5MP selfie camera, and is powered by a 3000 mAh battery running Android 4.4.2 (KitKat).

The HP Elite x3 is a discontinued Windows 10 smartphone released in 2016. It features a 5.96-inch AMOLED display (1440 x 2560), a Snapdragon 820 chipset, 4GB RAM, and 64GB storage. Notable hardware includes an IP67 rating, MIL-STD-810G compliance, Bang & Olufsen stereo speakers, and Microsoft Continuum support. It is equipped with a 16MP main camera capable of 4K video, an 8MP selfie camera, and a 4150 mAh battery with wireless charging.

Released in November 2023, the Infinix Smart 8 is an entry-level smartphone priced around 100 EUR. It features a 6.6-inch 90Hz IPS LCD display, a Unisoc T606 octa-core processor, 8GB of RAM, and 128GB of internal storage. The device is equipped with a 13MP dual main camera, an 8MP selfie camera, and a large 5000 mAh battery with 10W wired charging. It runs on Android 13 and includes a side-mounted fingerprint sensor.

Released in July 2022, the BLU Studio X5 Max is an entry-level Android 11 (Go edition) smartphone priced at approximately 50 EUR. It features a 5.7-inch IPS LCD display, 32GB internal storage with 2GB RAM, and a quad-core 1.3 GHz processor. The device includes a 5MP dual main camera, a 2MP selfie camera, and is powered by a removable 2700 mAh battery.

The Oukitel RT6 is a rugged 10.1-inch Android 13 tablet released in July 2023, priced around 350 EUR. It is built for extreme durability with IP68/IP69K ratings, MIL-STD-810H compliance, and drop resistance. Its standout feature is a massive 20,000 mAh battery with 33W fast charging and reverse wired charging. Performance is driven by a Mediatek MT8788 chipset with 8GB RAM and 256GB UFS 2.1 storage. It features 16MP front and rear cameras and weighs 1030g.

The LeEco Le 2 is a discontinued Android 6.0 smartphone released in April 2016. It features a 5.5-inch IPS LCD display (1080 x 1920), 3GB of RAM with either 32GB or 64GB of internal storage, and is powered by either a Helio X20 or Snapdragon 652 chipset. Key hardware includes a 16MP main camera capable of 4K video, an 8MP selfie camera, and a 3000 mAh battery with 18W fast charging. Notable connectivity includes USB Type-C and an infrared port, though it lacks a 3.5mm headphone jack and NFC.

The Kyocera Hydro Life is a discontinued rugged smartphone released in August 2014. It is highly durable, featuring IP57 dust/water resistance and MIL-STD-810G compliance. The device has a 4.5-inch IPS LCD (540x960), runs Android 4.3 on a Snapdragon 200 quad-core processor, and includes 8GB of storage with 1.5GB of RAM. It is equipped with a 5MP main camera, a 2MP selfie camera, and a removable 2000 mAh battery.

The Icemobile Prime 5.5 is a discontinued Android 4.4.2 (KitKat) smartphone released in August 2014. It features a 5.46-inch IPS LCD display (720x1280), a quad-core 1.3 GHz CPU, 1GB of RAM, and 16GB of internal storage. It is equipped with an 8MP main camera, a 2MP selfie camera, and a removable 2250 mAh battery.

Released in September 2023, the Blackview A96 is a budget-friendly smartphone priced at approximately $161.69. It features a 6.5-inch 120Hz IPS LCD display and is powered by a Mediatek Helio G99 chipset with options for 8GB or 12GB of RAM and 256GB of UFS 2.2 storage. The device includes a dual rear camera setup led by a 48MP wide lens and a 16MP selfie camera. It runs Android 13 and is supported by a 4380 mAh battery with 18W wired charging.

The Energizer U680S is a budget-friendly Android 11 smartphone released in Q3 2021, priced at approximately 120 EUR. Its standout feature is a massive 7000 mAh battery, paired with a large 6.8-inch IPS LCD display. It is powered by a Unisoc SC9863A octa-core chipset with 2GB RAM and 32GB of internal storage. The device includes a triple main camera setup led by a 13MP sensor and a 5MP selfie camera.

The Ericsson A2628 is a discontinued GSM phone released in 2001. It features a basic monochrome graphic display with 4 lines of resolution, no camera, and no modern connectivity like Bluetooth or WLAN. Key features include SMS messaging, WAP 1.1, and three built-in games. It is powered by a removable NiMH battery, with high-capacity options extending talk time up to 11 hours.

Released in February 2023, the Lava Yuva 2 Pro is an affordable Android 12 smartphone priced at ₹6,620. It features a 6.52-inch IPS LCD display, a Mediatek Helio G37 octa-core processor, 4GB of RAM, and 64GB of internal storage. The device is equipped with a triple main camera setup led by a 13MP sensor, a 5MP selfie camera, and a large 5000 mAh battery with 10W wired charging.

The Meizu 18s Pro is a 5G smartphone released in September 2021, featuring a high-resolution 6.7-inch Super AMOLED display with a 120Hz refresh rate and Gorilla Glass 6 protection. It is powered by the Snapdragon 888+ 5G chipset, offering memory options up to 12GB RAM and 256GB UFS 3.1 storage. The device boasts a triple main camera system led by a 50MP wide sensor with OIS and 8K video capabilities, complemented by a 44MP selfie camera. It includes a 4500 mAh battery supporting 40W wired and wireless charging, an ultrasonic under-display fingerprint sensor, and stereo speakers, priced at approximately 610 EUR.

The Spice Smart Flo 508 (Mi-508) is a discontinued Android 4.4.2 smartphone released in August 2014. It features a 5.0-inch IPS LCD display, a dual-core 1.3 GHz processor, and limited memory with 512MB RAM and 4GB internal storage. For photography, it has an 8MP main camera and a 2MP selfie camera. The device is powered by a 2000 mAh battery and supports dual SIM (GSM) connectivity.

The VK Mobile VK300 is a discontinued GSM phone released in July 2005. It is a compact, lightweight device (75g) featuring a 1.8-inch TFT display and a secondary external STN display. Lacking a camera, Bluetooth, WLAN, and USB, it provides basic functionality including SMS/MMS, a WAP 1.2.1 browser, and a removable 720 mAh battery offering up to 3 hours of talk time.
